q: The focus position or Z-position is not matching when running z-stack experiment or something seems like it isn't responding to the right z-value.
a: Go to the Devices menu bar on top and be sure you have the right z-device selected under z-device. Typically, you want this to be set to Ti2 Drive, depending on the microscope body. Make sure this same device is selected in the Z-stack setup experiment.

q: The focus knob indicator number or the Z-position value is not changing when using physical focus knobs.  I can't get the objective to move up or down.
a: If you can't change the z-value using the focus knobs or you can't move the lens up or down, it probably means the escape button was pressed by the last user. The escape and release buttons are on the software acquisition panel.

q: The stage seems to be not moving very fast or is not moving at all when I use the joystick.
a: If the joystick seems to not be moving and yet the lights are on the joystick, it is probably set to move excruciatingly slow. On a Nikon Ti2 (white/black microscope body), click the z button that has 3 up-arrows above it. It cycles through extra fine, fine, and coarse.

q: What should I do if I'm having issues getting good brightfield, transmitted, phase contrast, or DIC images?
a: If you are having trouble with Kohler illumination, first, move to a low magnification objective like 10x and focus using the eyepiece. While looking through the eyepiece, close the top diaphragm slowly until you see the iris leaves or dimming. Focus the condenser lens up and down until the dark/bright contrast is sharp. Center the spotlight using two Allen wrenches on the front of the condenser, then open the top iris until your field of view is just filled. Then you can move to a higher magnification.

q: How can I use the mouse to control focus and the Z-axis?
a: In Nikon Elements, go to the Devices menu, find Mouse Joystick Setup, and choose Ti2 Z-drive. Then, go to Devices again and click Enable Mouse Joystick. To control the XY stage movement with the mouse, click the compass-like icon at the top of a Live image window. Note that these controls only work when an image is live and not frozen.
